channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: COSS Weekly: CopilotKit raises $27M, SAP's COSS shopping spree, Anaconda acquires Outerbounds, and more
item.description: This week in COSS: CopilotKit raised a $27M Series A, SAP announced plans to acquire both Dremio and Prior Labs, Anaconda acquired Outerbounds, IREN acquired OpenStack champion Mirantis, DeepSeek...

item.title: Apple brings encrypted RCS chats to iPhone
item.description: Apple now lets you have encrypted RCS conversations with Android users through the Messages app on iOS. As part of iOS 26.5, which was released on Monday, Apple added support for end-to-end...

item.title: cPanel CVE-2026-41940 Under Active Exploitation to Deploy Filemanager Backdoor
item.description: A threat actor named Mr_Rot13 has been attributed to the exploitation of a recently disclosed critical cPanel flaw to deploy a backdoor codenamed Filemanager on compromised environments. The...

item.title: The Reply Was Never Random
item.description: [First published in Medium under the same title] (Feluda read crime scenes. LLMs read sentences. The geometry was always the same.) Long time no see. Have you been well? It’s me again, the coder...

item.title: Day 4: Leveling Up with Lists and Randomness in Python 🐍
item.description: I just wrapped up Day 4 of my Python journey! Today was all about moving beyond simple scripts and learning how to handle data more dynamically. It was a challenging day, but seeing the logic come...

item.title: Matching frontier LLMs at 22 lower latency: a 184M-parameter intent classifier for healthcare text
item.description: Healthcare practices drown in inbound patient text. Email, contact forms, live chat, SMS, voicemail transcripts — every channel sends messages that need to be routed: to scheduling, to billing, to...

item.title: Bild AI (YC W25) Is Hiring Founding Product Engineers
item.description: Article URL: https://bild.ai/jobs Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48098122 Points: 0 # Comments: 0

item.title: Secure Your Go Apps Before Production Does It For You
item.description: Hello, I'm Shrijith Venkatramana. I'm building git-lrc, an AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. Star Us to help devs discover the project. Do give it a try and share your feedback for...

item.title: Beyond MCP: Handling 845 Tools with 92% less context bloat via Elemm
item.description: Hi everyone, I’ve been diving deep into how AIs interact with tools and quickly hit a wall with the Model Context Protocol (MCP). As soon as you build complex, real-world toolsets, MCP becomes...

item.title: Inner vs Outer Joins: The Two Fundamental Join Types in SQL
item.description: SQL joins fall into two fundamental categories — inner joins and outer joins — while LEFT, RIGHT, and FULL joins are subclasses of the outer join. This hierarchy is defined by the logical...

item.title: How /search and /ask Work: Local Hybrid RAG with ChromaDB + SQLite FTS5
item.description: This is the second article in a five-part series about building Llamail, a private local AI email agent. In the first article, I showed the whole system behind it: Gmail, Telegram, n8n, FastAPI,...

item.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. A few months before he was awarded the Nobel Prize in...

item.title: CI/CD Auto-Remediation: The Complete Guide for SRE and Platform Teams (2026)
item.description: Key Takeaways Most teams do not yet auto-remediate inside CI/CD. Per JetBrains' AI Pulse coverage (April 2026), 78.2% of respondents don't use AI in CI/CD workflows at all — even though AI is now...

item.title: Texas sues Netflix for advertising ‘bait and switch’ and spying
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton has filed a lawsuit against Netflix, accusing the company of turning its back on its promise to remain ad-free and safe for kids. The lawsuit, filed on Monday,...

item.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
item.description: Digg returns (again) as another place to read AI news.

item.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
item.description: For the first time, Google says it has spotted and stopped a zero-day exploit developed with AI. According to a report from Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG), "prominent cyber crime threat...

item.title: A million baby monitors and security cameras were easily viewable by hackers
item.description: A baby's eyes peer directly into the camera lens. A kid with a striped shirt looks up, then away. A boy in a policeman's costume, a gold star on his chest. A messy bedroom that reminds me of my...

item.title: 7 of our favorite deals from Amazon’s Pet Days sale
item.description: A big part of my job here at The Verge is to find deals on tech that readers will like. But in my personal life, I’m not spending anywhere near as much money on tech as I am on food, litter, and...

item.title: Hackers Used AI to Develop First Known Zero-Day 2FA Bypass for Mass Exploitation
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that it identified an unknown threat actor using a zero-day exploit that it said was likely developed with an artificial intelligence (AI) system, marking the first time...

item.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: GM settles California lawsuit claiming it sold driving habit data to insurance companies
item.description: General Motors has agreed to pay $12.75 million to settle a California data privacy lawsuit that accused the automaker of selling driver location and driver data, as reported earlier by Reuters....

item.title: Matter and OpenADR team up to connect smart homes to the grid
item.description: Smart energy management just took a step closer to becoming simpler. This week, the organizations behind Matter, the smart-home interoperability standard, and the OpenADR protocol, which sends...

item.title: Joanna Stern is not a robot, but she lived with them
item.description: My guest today is longtime friend of the show Joanna Stern. You all know Joanna: she is the former senior personal technology columnist for The Wall Street Journal, a former Decoder guest host,...

item.title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
item.description: Despite years of digitization, organizations capture less than one-third of the value expected from digital investments, according to McKinsey research. That’s because most big companies begin...

item.title: TikTok is letting UK users pay to remove ads
item.description: TikTok is preparing to roll out a paid, ad-free version of its app in the UK. The new subscription announced today will be available to users over 18 "over the coming months," according to...

item.title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
item.description: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth,...

item.title: Innovation abounds in device charging
item.description: The changes may be less perceptible than in smartphones, tablets, or wearables, but chargers have also been quietly reinvented over the last decade. At one time a bulky mix of tangled cables and...

item.title: Implementing advanced AI technologies in finance
item.description: In finance departments that have long been defined by precision and control, AI has arrived less as a neatly managed upgrade than as a quiet insurgency. Employees are already using it while...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Rootkit, macOS Crypto Stealer, WebSocket Skimmers and More
item.description: Rough Monday. Somebody poisoned a trusted download again, somebody else turned cloud servers into public housing, and a few crews are still getting into boxes with bugs that should’ve died years...

item.title: The Download: the hantavirus outbreak and Musk v. Altman week 2
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship...
